examples.title: Truth Table Generator for 2-Input Logic Gates
examples.description: Python program to generate truth tables for all basic 2-input logic gates
examples.code:
def generate_truth_table():
    print("A B | AND OR NOT-A NOT-B NAND NOR XOR XNOR")
    print("-" * 50)
    
    for A in [0, 1]:
        for B in [0, 1]:
            and_out = A and B
            or_out = A or B
            not_a = not A
            not_b = not B
            nand_out = not (A and B)
            nor_out = not (A or B)
            xor_out = (A and not B) or (not A and B)
            xnor_out = not xor_out
            
            print(f"{A} {B} |  {int(and_out)}  {int(or_out)}    {int(not_a)}    )}    {int {int(not_b(nand_out)}  {int(nor_out)}  {int(xor_out)}  {int(xnor_out)}")

generate_truth_table()
examples.explanation: This program generates complete truth tables for all 2-input basic logic gates by iterating through all 4 possible input combinations (00, 01, 10, 11) and computing each gate's output.

examples.title: Boolean Expression Evaluator
examples.description: C program to evaluate Boolean expressions for combinational logic
examples.explanation: This C program implements basic logic gates as functions and evaluates the Boolean function F = A + B.C (OR of A and AND of B,C). It generates 8 rows for all 3-input combinations.

examples.title: 2-to-1 Multiplexer Implementation
examples.description: Pseudocode showing how a multiplexer selects input based on select line
examples.explanation: The multiplexer selects between two data inputs based on the select line. When S=0, output equals input A; when S=1, output equals input B. This demonstrates how combinational logic performs data selection.

examples.title: 3-to-8 Decoder Implementation
examples.description: Python program demonstrating decoder as combinational circuit
examples.code:
def decoder_3to8(A, B, C):
    """
    3-to-8 Decoder using combinational logic
    Inputs: A, B, C (3-bit binary address)
    Outputs: D0 to D7 (one-hot output)
    """
    # Each output is HIGH for one specific input combination
    outputs = []
    
    # Generate all 8 outputs using NOT and AND gates
    # D0 = A'.B'.C' (when inputs = 000)
    d0 = (not A) and (not B) and (not C)
    # D1 = A'.B'.C  (when inputs = 001)
    d1 = (not A) and (not B) and C
    # D2 = A'.B.C'  (when inputs = 010)
    d2 = (not A) and B and (not C)
    # D3 = A'.B.C   (when inputs = 011)
    d3 = (not A) and B and C
    # D4 = A.B'.C'  (when inputs = 100)
    d4 = A and (not B) and (not C)
    # D5 = A.B'.C   (when inputs = 101)
    d5 = A and (not B) and C
    # D6 = A.B.C'   (when inputs = 110)
    d6 = A and B and (not C)
    # D7 = A.B.C    (when inputs = 111)
    d7 = A and B and C
    
    return [int(d0), int(d1), int(d2), int(d3), int(d4), int(d5), int(d6), int(d7)]

# Test the decoder
print("A B C | D0 D1 D2 D3 D4 D5 D6 D7")
print("-" * 45)
for A in [0, 1]:
    for B in [0, 1]:
        for C in [0, 1]:
            outputs = decoder_3to8(A, B, C)
            print(f"{A} {B} {C} |  {outputs[0]}  {outputs[1]}  {outputs[2]}  {outputs[3]}  {outputs[4]}  {outputs[5]}  {outputs[6]}  {outputs[7]}")
examples.explanation: A 3-to-8 decoder converts a 3-bit binary input into one of 8 mutually exclusive outputs. Exactly one output is HIGH (1) for each input combination, representing the binary value decoded. This is a fundamental combinational circuit used in memory addressing and data routing.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - fixed output array of size 8
